edges.description: HPV-associated oncogenesis independently converges on malignant squamous proliferation rather than causing the somatic driver alterations enriched in HPV-negative tumors.
HPV status stratifies TMB and immune-checkpoint biomarker patterns.
Driver alterations support malignant proliferation and invasion.

evidence.reference_title: Comprehensive genomic profiling of penile squamous cell carcinoma and the impact of human papillomavirus status on immune-checkpoint inhibitor-related biomarkers.
evidence.snippet: CDKN2A mutations (0% vs. 37.5%) were exclusive to HPV16/18- tumors.

nodes.description: Persistent high-risk HPV infection promotes malignant transformation of penile squamous epithelium and creates an HPV-associated PSCC subset with distinct immune-checkpoint biomarker features.

evidence.reference_title: HPV and Penile Cancer: Epidemiology, Risk Factors, and Clinical Insights.
evidence.snippet: Approximately 40% of penile tumors are associated with human papillomavirus (HPV) infection.

evidence.reference_title: Comprehensive genomic profiling of penile squamous cell carcinoma and the impact of human papillomavirus status on immune-checkpoint inhibitor-related biomarkers.
evidence.snippet: Approximately half of pSCC cases are associated with human papillomavirus (HPV) infection.

nodes.description: Checkpoint-inhibitor combinations are investigational for advanced PSCC and include pembrolizumab-containing regimens motivated by PD-L1/TMB biomarker findings and early clinical trial experience.

evidence.reference_title: Therapeutic Targets in Advanced Penile Cancer: From Bench to Bedside
evidence.snippet: We conclude that antibody-drug conjugates are especially promising, as are the combinations of immune checkpoint inhibitors with other types of drugs.

evidence.reference_title: A Phase II Trial of Pembrolizumab Combined With Cisplatin-based Chemotherapy as First-line Systemic Therapy in Advanced Penile Cancer
evidence.snippet: This is a phase II clinical trial evaluating activity, safety and patients reported outcomes of first-line pembrolizumab plus cisplatin (or carboplatin) plus 5-FU for patients with advanced penile squamous cell carcinoma.

nodes.description: A substantial subset of PSCC shows PD-L1 positivity and high tumor mutational burden, particularly in HPV-positive tumors, providing a biomarker rationale for immune-checkpoint inhibitor trials.

biological_processes: negative regulation of T cell mediated immunity

evidence.reference_title: Comprehensive genomic profiling of penile squamous cell carcinoma and the impact of human papillomavirus status on immune-checkpoint inhibitor-related biomarkers.
evidence.snippet: Overall, 51% of tumors were PD-L1+, 10.7% had high TMB, and 1.1% had mismatch repair-deficient (dMMR)/MSI-high status.

evidence.reference_title: Comprehensive genomic profiling of penile squamous cell carcinoma and the impact of human papillomavirus status on immune-checkpoint inhibitor-related biomarkers.
evidence.snippet: Increased tumor mutational burden is associated with HPV-positive tumors, and could serve as a biomarker for predicting therapeutic response to ICI-based therapies.

nodes.description: Transformed penile squamous cells proliferate locally and may disseminate to inguinal lymph nodes, making nodal assessment central to staging and prognosis.

evidence.reference_title: A comprehensive review of current knowledge on penile squamous cell carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Lymph node involvement is a common finding at first presentation and investigation of spread to deep nodes is important and can be done with the aid of PET-CT.

evidence.reference_title: Minimally Invasive Management of Inguinal Lymph Nodes in Penile Cancer: Recent Progress and Remaining Challenges.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of occult inguinal lymph node metastasis in clinically node-negative invasive penile squamous cell carcinoma (PSCC) has remained a challenge, with substantial perioperative complications.

evidence.reference_title: Comprehensive genomic profiling of penile squamous cell carcinoma and the impact of human papillomavirus status on immune-checkpoint inhibitor-related biomarkers.
evidence.snippet: revealed TP53 (46%), CDKN2A (26%), and PIK3CA (25%) to be the most common mutations.

nodes.description: PSCC commonly carries somatic alterations in TP53, CDKN2A, and PIK3CA, disrupting checkpoint control and growth/survival signaling in transformed penile squamous cells.

evidence.snippet: revealed TP53 (46%), CDKN2A (26%), and PIK3CA (25%) to be the most common mutations.
